text: The surgeon mobilizes the mesocolon along a dissection plane within the taut fascia, exploring the duodenal mesenteric fossa. The mesenteric vein is identified at the lower border of the pancreas, followed by dissection inside the visceral peritoneum, medial to the inferior mesenteric vein.

The surgeon creates a retroperitoneal tunnel along the retroperitoneal fascia plane by tenting the mesentery. The inferior mesenteric vein is clipped and transected, followed by sweeping down the widest gyrota fascia to separate it from the overlying mesocolon.

The surgeon continues dissection along the taut fascia plane, progressing from medial to lateral direction. The dissection advances over the surface of the pancreas tail, leading to entry into the lesser sac.

The surgeon develops the dissection plane laterally, incising the peritoneum over the bifurcation area of the abdominal aorta. The mesocolon, containing the superior rectal artery, is tented to create a retroperitoneal tunnel from medial to lateral.

The surgeon continues to develop the dissection plane downward, transecting the vascular pedicle below the branching of the left colic artery. Continuous tenting and traction of the mesenteric vascular pedicle facilitate the downward progression of the fascia dissection.

The ureter and gonadal vessel are clearly visible and well preserved. At the sacral promontory, the taut fascia is observed to end and fuse with the proper fascia of the rectum and the endopelvic fascia.

Following the incision of the fused fascia, the surgeon enters the presacral space. The dissection is then shifted to the left lateral peritoneal reflection, revealing the anatomical structures in this area.

The surgeon separates the lateral colonic attachment along the white line of Toldt. As the peritoneal reflection is tented, the Toldt fascia is visible tapering and becoming more defined at this spot.

The surgeon continues the incision of peritoneal attachments along the white line of Toldt, extending upward to the lower border of the spleen and the tail of the pancreas. The left side colon is completely mobilized along the Toldt fascia.
